x-enumeration-literals.name: Periodic Repayment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayments are made at regular intervals throughout the loan term.

Examples:
Monthly payments: A personal loan repaid monthly for 36 months.
Quarterly payments
Annual payments

x-enumeration-literals.name: Maturity Repayment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Lump-Sum (Maturity) Repayment Timing: The entire repayment occurs at the end of the loan term.

Comment:
Example:
A short-term loan where the borrower repays the full amount after 12 months.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Grace Period Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayments start after a specified delay following loan disbursement.

Comment:
Example:
A student loan where repayments begin 6 months after graduation.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Balloon Payment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayments occur periodically, but a large final payment is due at the end of the loan term.

Comment:
Example:
A business loan with small monthly payments and a large final payment after 5 years.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Step Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment timing or frequency changes at predefined stages during the loan term.

Comment:
Example:
Quarterly payments during the first year and monthly payments afterward.

x-enumeration-literals.name: On-Demand Repayment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment must be made whenever the lender requests it.

Comment:
Example:
Certain overdraft facilities where the bank may demand repayment at any time.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Event-Based Repayment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Repayment is triggered by a specific event rather than a fixed schedule.

Comment:
Examples:
Sale of collateral
Completion of a project
Receipt of business revenue

x-enumeration-literals.name: Flexible Repayment Timing
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Borrowers can repay at any time within the credit term, provided they meet minimum repayment requirements.

Comment:
Example:
Credit card repayments where the borrower can repay any amount above the minimum at any time.
